name: /payments lights up with real data — hero + sparkline + top payors
description: An account with real payment history. PaymentsHero shows landed totals; PSideFlow draws a real cash-flow shape; PSideTopPayors ranks the top 4 clients; PSideMix slices method share. The whole page should read as a snapshot of a healthy month, not as KPI plumbing.

persona.name: Diego Martínez
persona.context: End of April. Wants to know which clients actually paid and which method dominated.

preconditions: Account has 12+ landed payments across the last 8 weeks
Mix of methods: ACH (~60%), card (~25%), check (~15%)

steps.expect: Hero reads '<X> landed this month' as a real money figure (cents-correct, no $0)
Cash-flow sparkline draws as a curve with 12 weekly points; the right-most dot is larger; subtitle reads 'Last 12 weeks · $X,XXX this week'
Top 4 clients listed by total paid; bar widths scale to the leader; first-place rank is '01'
Donut/legend shows non-zero method slices only; ACH/card/check colors match the design system
Subtle dot enlargement; cursor remains default (no broken tooltip)

evaluationCriteria.check: All four right-rail cards have visual rhythm; no card looks empty-but-rendered
Reads as 'this month was real' — not as scaffolding
Money formatting uses fmtMoney (no cents) for KPIs and fmtMoneyExact (cents) for receipt totals; no '$NaN'
SVG sparkline has role='img' with an aria-label summarizing the trend; client-name list is a real <ul>
